ksa_text: Design and lead enterprise data and AI transformation programs, including operating model redesign, organizational change management planning, capability development roadmaps, and transformation team leadership.

dcwf.top_element_label: Collaborate with stakeholders to identify and/or develop appropriate solutions technology.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of system design tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Leadership & Strategy KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.442) to justify inclusion for the AI CoE Leader. Without stronger framework alignment, this task KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain task KSA be expected in real-world AI CoE Leader roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm Leadership & Strategy is not a standard competency expectation for AI CoE Leader. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the AI CoE Leader competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.442) and edge-domain placement (Leadership & Strategy) do not meet inclusion threshold for the AI CoE Leader. All passes support exclusion.
